title: Resolve config by documented precedence
description: When the same setting can come from flags, env vars, files, and defaults, resolve and report the effective value by the documented precedence order instead of guessing the source.
body: Use when a setting could originate from multiple layers (CLI flag, environment variable, .env, config file, built-in default) and behavior depends on which wins. Do not assume a value from the file is active when an env var overrides it, and avoid editing the wrong layer. Actions: enumerate every layer that defines the key, apply the documented precedence (usually flag > env > file > default), and record the resolved effective value plus its winning source. Validation signals include printing the effective config at startup and a test asserting an env override beats the file. Risk boundary: if precedence is undocumented or ambiguous, surface the conflict rather than silently picking one; escalate when a security-relevant setting resolves unexpectedly.
